steps.title: Factor the denominator
steps.content:
Factor the second denominator so both fractions can use the same denominator.
$$x^2y-7xy=xy(x-7)$$

steps.title: Use a common denominator
steps.content:
Multiply the first fraction by $\frac{xy}{xy}$, which equals $1$.
$$\frac{y+10}{x-7}=\frac{xy(y+10)}{xy(x-7)}$$

steps.title: Combine the numerators
steps.content:
Now add the numerators over the common denominator.
$$\frac{xy(y+10)+y(x-7)}{xy(x-7)}$$
$$xy(y+10)+y(x-7)=xy^2+10xy+xy-7y$$
$$xy^2+10xy+xy-7y=xy^2+11xy-7y$$

steps.title: Match the final form
steps.content:
The denominator $xy(x-7)$ is the same as $x^2y-7xy$.
$$\frac{xy^2+11xy-7y}{x^2y-7xy}$$
The answer is C.
